title: Sleep Has a Memory Assembly Line. Scientists Decoded the Shift Schedule.
description: Sleep researchers have cracked the exact oscillatory choreography — a three-way handshake between slow waves, spindles, and hippocampal ripples — that determines whether a memory survives the night. NREM and REM sleep don't do the same job twice: they push memory representations in opposite directions, and disrupting either stage has consequences that compound across a lifetime. The intervention tools are real but early-stage. This is not a story about sleep hygiene. It's a story about what your brain is actually doing while you're unconscious.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Your Brain Runs a Memory Factory at Night. The Shift Schedule Is More Precise Than Anyone Knew.
